proved: A host session opens only from a runtime-valid strict manifest, an explicit unique grant subset, and a bounded project/revision/source snapshot.
Unknown manifest, runtime, permission, contribution, and proposal fields fail closed in the in-process validator, including malformed untyped proposal fields.
Sessions expire after 15 minutes, can be explicitly revoked, and return content-free typed failure codes.
Project reads require project.read and return detached copies that cannot mutate the broker's stored snapshot.
Project proposals require project.propose, valid bounded content, exact plugin/project identity, and the captured revision, and return only pending-human-review without changing the snapshot.

Network decisions require network.fetch, an explicitly granted exact/wildcard domain, HTTPS on the default HTTPS port, no URL credentials, no literal IP/local/internal/single-label host, a one-MiB ceiling, and an explicit downstream public-address recheck flag.
Model decisions require model.invoke and an explicitly granted provider; remote decisions require disclosure and every decision requires a provider-run record.
Drive decisions require the matching read/propose capability, exact selected-workspace identity, and a downstream target recheck flag.
The broker imports no Tauri invoke boundary and performs no fetch, provider generation, Drive operation, plugin load, or project mutation.
The compiled MCP reports the broker as implemented-non-executing while pluginLoader remains contract-only.
notProved: A WASI component parses, instantiates, runs, traps, times out, or receives only linked host capabilities.
A native MCP plugin process is installed, warned, hashed, launched, constrained, or terminated.
A downstream network host resolves DNS, rejects private/link-local results, rechecks redirects, enforces response bytes, or sanitizes content.
A provider or Drive host consumes a broker decision without target drift or authority expansion.
A proposal diff is rendered, accepted, rejected, attributed, or applied with a live revision recheck.
Plugin discovery, install/disable/upgrade/removal, contribution UI, publication, signatures, runtime safety, or usefulness.
